weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Last hypertrophy week — we keep the volume but nudge a little heavier and a little lower in reps to set up the strength block ahead. Everything is fresh this week; read the cues and hold the line at 3 sets. Earn it with a bit more load or a cleaner rep, not an added set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 18 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · drive up with intent on every rep
patterns.load: Your competition KB, or 1 size heavier, at competition box height
cues: Choose the heavier KB only if you can still hit all 18 reps with full standups.
Sit to the box and stand fast — push the floor away on every rep.
Keep your heels flat and your knees out over your toes throughout.
Brace your belly tight before each rep so your back stays solid.

patterns.name: Heavy Goblet Hold Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 2 min between sets · pause 2 seconds at the bottom of each rep
patterns.load: A KB 1–2 sizes HEAVIER than competition that you barely finish at 10 reps
cues: Pick a bell heavy enough that rep 10 is a real struggle.
Hold the bottom for a full 2 seconds with your chest tall before standing.
Keep both elbows inside your knees at the bottom to stay upright.
Stop the set if your back rounds — finish strong, not sloppy.

patterns.name: KB Cossack Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps each side · rest 75 sec between sets · shift side to side under control
patterns.load: A light KB held at the chest, or bodyweight if balance is the limit
cues: Sit down over one bent leg while the other leg stays straight, toes up.
Keep the bent-leg heel flat on the floor the whole time.
Go only as deep as you can control — do not flop into the bottom.
Press the floor away to come back to center between sides.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 throws · rest 90 sec between sets · 12 throws, a touch more effort than smooth practice
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Hinge low, then explode up and throw the ball up and back over your head with a touch more effort than smooth practice.
All the speed comes from snapping your hips open; your arms just steer the ball overhead.
Take full rest between sets so every throw is fresh and fast, not tired.
Reset your stance completely before each throw and keep your stomach braced through the finish.

patterns.name: KB Sumo Deadlift
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A KB 1–2 sizes up from your swing bell, e.g. 16–24 kg — heavy enough to challenge you by rep 10
cues: Stand wide with toes turned out, hinge down to grab the bell between your feet, then stand tall by driving through your heels and squeezing your glutes.
Keep your chest up and back flat — push the floor away with your legs rather than yanking with your back.
Lower under control and lightly tap the floor between reps; don't bounce the bell.
Stop the set if your back rounds or your knees cave inward.

patterns.name: Seated DB Overhead Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A pair of DBs a step heavier than a 12-rep weight, still clean for 10 reps
cues: Sit tall against an upright bench and press both DBs overhead until your arms lock out, then lower to your shoulders under control.
Brace your stomach so your low back stays against the bench — the seat keeps you honest, no leaning back.
Drive the weights up with intent and lower them slowly.
Stop the set the moment you can't lock out cleanly overhead.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~60% of your max-rep test · rest 90 sec between · slow 3-sec lower on every rep
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — hit it on all 3 sets, taking a slow 3-second count to lower the bar each rep.
Press back up at a normal speed after the slow lower, keeping the touch on your chest light.
End the set when you can no longer control the 3-second lower or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Floor DB Tricep Extension
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Two light-to-moderate DBs you can control for 12 reps
cues: Lie on your back, press the DBs up, then bend only at the elbows to lower them toward your forehead.
Keep your upper arms pointing straight up and still — only the forearms move.
Extend back to a full lockout and squeeze the triceps. Stop if your elbows drift apart.

patterns.name: Decline Push-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight with your hands on a low bench or sturdy box
cues: Place both hands on a bench so your body is on a downhill angle, then lower your chest to the bench.
Press to a full lockout keeping your body in one straight line from head to heels.
If 12 is too easy, slow the lower to a 3-count; if too hard, raise the hands higher. End the set when your hips sag.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min 30 sec between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — slightly fuller holds, still step down at 80%
cues: Pack the shoulders and stay active from the first second to the last.
Grip hard with spread fingers and keep your breathing slow and even.
Step down under control the moment your hand starts to give.
Record the hold time so the app can track where you are.

patterns.name: Fat-Grip Dumbbell Hold
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: A heavy dumbbell in each hand on fat grips — pick a weight your grip can only hold 20–40 seconds
cues: Stand tall and just hold the dumbbells at your sides — let the open-hand grip do the work.
Squeeze the fat grips like you are trying to leave a fingerprint in them.
When the weight starts to slide, set it down safely — that time is your number.
Keep your shoulders pulled back and down so you do not slump forward.

patterns.name: Chest-Supported Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Dumbbells heavier than a higher-rep set — pick a weight that is hard but clean by rep 10
cues: Lie chest-down on an incline bench so your torso cannot cheat the pull.
Row the dumbbells to your hips and pinch your shoulder blades hard at the top.
Lower all the way until your arms hang straight and your back stretches.
Keep every rep strict at this lower-rep, heavier weight.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball, or one size heavier if it stays crisp
cues: Reach fully overhead and slam hard — make all 12 reps sharp and full-effort.
Reset cleanly between reps; quality of the slam beats piling up sloppy reps.
If you size up the ball, stop the set the moment your overhead reach shortens.

patterns.name: Half-Kneeling D-Ball Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps each side · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A moderate D-ball, 10 lb (women) / 20 lb (men)
cues: One knee down, one foot forward; slam straight down past your front foot, then switch sides each set.
Keep your hips square and tall so the work stays in your trunk and arms.
Control the ball down to a stop — no flopping forward over your front knee.

patterns.name: Dumbbell Bent-Over Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each arm · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A dumbbell you can row for 12 strong reps per arm
cues: Hinge with a flat back, row the dumbbell to your hip, then lower it all the way down.
Pull with your back, not just your arm; keep your shoulders level the whole set.
This builds the upper-back strength that powers the slam's finish.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · rest 90 sec between sets · slightly quicker than easy pace
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at a brisk but controlled comp pace
cues: Pick up the turnover a touch from a comfortable pace, but keep it one you can hold for 60 seconds.
Keep your elbows tucked near your ribs so the wrists do the spinning.
Breathe in a steady rhythm rather than holding your breath.

patterns.name: Bent-Knee Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · 1-sec hold at the top
patterns.load: Hold a moderate-to-heavy KB at your chest, knees softly bent throughout
cues: Keep a slight bend in your knees the whole set to hit the lower calf.
Rise onto the balls of your feet and squeeze, then lower under control.
Don't let the knees straighten and re-bend — hold the bend steady.

patterns.name: Pogo Hops
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 hops · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, light fingertip support on a rail if needed
cues: Hop straight up and down on both feet with a stiff, springy ankle.
Stay over the same spot and keep the hops small and controlled.
Touch a rail with your fingertips only for balance — don't pull on it.
If you want more challenge and your balance is solid, hop on one foot at a time, but keep both-foot hops if standing alone.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 jumps · rest 90 sec between sets · a touch more reach, still controlled
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Swing your arms back, then jump forward with a little more intent than easy — but still stick both feet on landing.
Bend the knees and hips to soak up each landing quietly; a sloppy landing means you reached too far.
Reset fully at the line between every jump. Quality landings come first, distance second.

patterns.name: Tempo Goblet Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · 3-sec lower, 1-sec pause at the bottom
patterns.load: A KB or DB at your chest, a touch heavier than a comfortable 12-rep weight so 10 controlled reps are challenging
cues: Lower slowly for a 3-count, pause at the bottom with your chest up, then stand by driving through your heels.
Keep the bell tight to your chest and your knees tracking over your toes — don't let them collapse inward.
Sit to a depth your knees and hips allow comfortably. Stop the set if your back rounds at the bottom.

patterns.name: Hip Thrust off Bench
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · 2-sec squeeze at the top
patterns.load: Bodyweight, or rest a single DB across your hips, heavy enough that 12 reps are hard
cues: Rest your upper back on a bench, feet flat, and drive your hips up until your body forms a straight line.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top and tuck your ribs down so your lower back doesn't over-arch.
Lower under control and tap the hips low before the next rep. Drop the weight if your back takes over.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 500m · rest 3 min fully between · last piece a touch faster than the first two
patterns.load: Rower · about 3–4 sec/500m slower than all-out on pieces one and two, then a hair quicker on three
cues: Hold a controlled split on the first two, then add a little leg drive on the third piece only.
Finish each stroke with the handle at the bottom of the ribs and elbows past your sides.
If your split jumps early, ease off — you want to speed up on the last piece, not survive it.

patterns.name: Inverted Bar Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight under a fixed waist-high bar · raise the bar to make it easier if 12 is too hard
cues: Lie under a fixed bar, keep your body in one straight line, and pull your chest up to the bar.
Squeeze the shoulder blades at the top, then lower yourself slowly with arms fully straight.
Raise the bar higher to make it lighter — never let your hips sag toward the floor.

patterns.name: Tempo Row Holds
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 strokes · rest 60 sec between sets · 3-count on the recovery
patterns.load: Rower · moderate effort with a deliberately slow 3-count slide forward
cues: Drive the stroke normally, then take a slow 3-count to slide back up to the catch.
This grooves a long, controlled recovery — don't rush forward off the finish.
Keep the legs flat and the handle level the whole way; reset cleanly each stroke.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 200-yd shuttle (4 × 50 yd) · rest 3 min between · slightly faster than easy pace
patterns.load: Bodyweight · push the pace a touch quicker than a comfortable jog while staying controlled
cues: Run four 50-yard lengths a notch quicker than a comfortable pace, still smooth through every turn.
Decelerate, plant low, and re-accelerate cleanly — control the turn so you stay safe alone.
Rest fully between sets so the slightly higher pace stays quality.

patterns.name: Goblet Squat to Stand
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · controlled down, firm up · rest 2 min
patterns.load: One dumbbell or kettlebell at the chest, ~25–40 lb — heavy enough that 12 is challenging
cues: Hold the weight at your chest, sit down between your knees to a comfortable depth, then stand tall.
Keep your heels down and chest up — drive the floor away to stand, don't lean forward.
Aim for 12 controlled reps at a weight heavy enough to bridge toward heavier work.

patterns.name: Curve Run Arcs
patterns.rx: 3 × 40 yards on a gentle curve · rest as needed · ~75% effort
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Run a wide, sweeping arc rather than a straight line, leaning gently into the curve.
Stay tall and relaxed at about three-quarter effort — this teaches your body to turn under speed.
Take full recovery so each curved run stays smooth and controlled.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 2 min between · strong and controlled (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load + 5 lb per side (women: 2 × 30 lb / men: 2 × 50 lb) · slight overload over comp
cues: At competition load plus 5 lb per side — push the full 50 yards strong and steady, still sled work and not a timed test.
Stay at about 45° with arms locked, driving through your trunk — don't bend your elbows to absorb the extra plate.
Long, powerful steps with full hip extension, driving through your toes the whole way.

patterns.name: Heels-Elevated Goblet Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · controlled tempo · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: One kettlebell or dumbbell at your chest, heels on a small plate, heavy enough that the last 2 reps are tough but clean
cues: With your heels raised slightly, sit straight down into a deep squat so your quads do the driving for sled power.
Keep the weight tight to your chest and your torso tall — don't let your back round at the bottom.
Stand all the way up and squeeze your legs at the top, and stop the set if your depth or posture slips.

patterns.name: Single-Arm DB Push Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps per arm · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: One dumbbell at shoulder height, heavy enough that the last rep per arm is a strong effort
cues: Dip slightly with your legs and drive the dumbbell overhead in one piece to train the full-body push that powers the sled.
Lock your arm out at the top with the weight stacked over your shoulder — don't let it drift forward.
Lower under control to your shoulder between reps, and brace your core so your back doesn't arch.
